summary:
The most likely cause of hypernatremia in this breastfed infant is severe dehydration secondary to gastrointestinal fluid loss, as evidenced by normal urinary concentration capability (urine Na+ = 70 mEq/L) and absence of signs typical of diabetes insipidus. This condition arises when insufficient oral hydration occurs despite adequate milk intake, commonly seen in infections like vomiting or diarrhea.  

(Note: Final choice confirmed as Option C.) 

Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$
